_meta.source: whitehouse.gov, official department websites, congress.gov confirmation records
_meta.notes: President record is type 'president' and always renders at top. All 15 Cabinet departments follow alphabetically by department_name. Contact URLs link to each department's official public contact page. Photo paths are relative to /assets/photos/cabinet/.

profile.bio: Donald J. Trump is the 47th President of the United States, having assumed office on January 20, 2025. He previously served as the 45th President from 2017 to 2021.
Brooke Rollins was confirmed as the 33rd Secretary of Agriculture on February 13, 2025. She previously served as Director of the Domestic Policy Council in the first Trump administration.
Howard Lutnick was confirmed as Secretary of Commerce on February 18, 2025. He previously served as CEO of Cantor Fitzgerald and co-led President Trump's transition team.

cabinet.department_name_note: Also referred to as the 'Department of War' under a 2025 executive order. The statutory name remains Department of Defense; a permanent rename is pending in Congress.
cabinet.title: Secretary of Defense
cabinet.title_note: Also referred to as 'Secretary of War' under the same executive order.

profile.bio: Pete Hegseth was confirmed as Secretary of Defense on January 24, 2025, with Vice President JD Vance casting the tie-breaking vote. He previously served as a Fox News host and is a U.S. Army National Guard veteran.

Linda McMahon was confirmed as Secretary of Education on March 3, 2025. She previously served as Administrator of the Small Business Administration during the first Trump administration and as co-founder of World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
Chris Wright was confirmed as Secretary of Energy on February 3, 2025. He previously served as CEO of Liberty Energy, a Denver-based oilfield services company.

cabinet.department_name: Department of Health and Human Services
cabinet.title: Secretary of Health and Human Services

profile.bio: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. was confirmed as the 26th Secretary of Health and Human Services on February 13, 2025, in a 52–48 Senate vote. He is the son of Senator Robert F. Kennedy and nephew of President John F. Kennedy, and previously founded the nonprofit Children's Health Defense.

Markwayne Mullin was confirmed as the 9th Secretary of Homeland Security on March 24, 2026, in a 54–45 vote. He previously represented Oklahoma in the U.S. House (2013–2023) and Senate (2023–2026), and is a member of the Cherokee Nation and former professional MMA fighter.
profile.notes: Mullin replaced Kristi Noem, who served as the first DHS Secretary of this administration.

cabinet.department_name: Department of Housing and Urban Development
cabinet.title: Secretary of Housing and Urban Development

profile.bio: Eric Scott Turner was confirmed as Secretary of Housing and Urban Development on February 5, 2025. He previously served in a White House policy role during the first Trump administration.

Doug Burgum was confirmed as Secretary of the Interior on January 30, 2025, in a 79–18 Senate vote. He previously served as Governor of North Dakota from 2016 to 2024 and was a candidate for the 2024 Republican presidential nomination.

Todd Blanche is serving as Acting Attorney General following the resignation of Pamela Bondi. He previously served as a federal prosecutor and was President Trump's personal defense attorney during the 2024 election.
profile.notes: Pamela Bondi served as the confirmed Attorney General from January 2025 until her resignation. Todd Blanche is serving in an acting capacity pending a new nomination.

profile.bio: Keith Sonderling is serving as Acting Secretary of Labor following the resignation of Lori Chavez-DeRemer. He previously served as a Commissioner and Acting Chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC).
profile.notes: Lori Chavez-DeRemer served as the confirmed Secretary of Labor until her resignation. Keith Sonderling is serving in an acting capacity pending a new nomination.

profile.bio: Marco Rubio was confirmed as Secretary of State on January 20, 2025, in a 99–0 Senate vote — the first Cabinet confirmation of the Trump administration. He previously represented Florida in the U.S. Senate from 2011 to 2025.
Sean Duffy was confirmed as Secretary of Transportation in 2025. He previously represented Wisconsin's 7th Congressional District in the U.S. House from 2011 to 2019.

Scott Bessent was confirmed as Secretary of the Treasury in January 2025. He previously founded Key Square Group, a global macro investment firm, and served as Chief Investment Officer at Soros Fund Management.

Doug Collins was confirmed as Secretary of Veterans Affairs in 2025. He previously represented Georgia's 9th Congressional District in the U.S. House from 2013 to 2021 and is a U.S. Air Force Reserve chaplain.
